title: Common component output contract v0.5
description: Fields every pipeline component's output JSON includes in common. Judgment-type outputs add the safety fields (source_grounded, hallucination_risk_check, prohibited_language_check). See harness-guardrails (P1) for the rules this schema enforces.

component.description: Name of the agent/component that produced this output. e.g. claim-analysis

source_grounded.description: Safety field (judgment-type outputs): whether every claim traces to a source quote. If false, review_required must be true (see harness-guardrails P1).

prohibited_language_check.description: Whether the output violates the forbidden-expression guide (see pipeline.md). CriticAgent performs the final check.

allOf.description: Rule: if source_grounded is false, review_required must be true.
Rule: if review_required is true, reviewer_role must be set.

reviewer_role.description: Kept in Korean -- these are the actual routing values used by the real-world review workflow (a Korean-speaking loss adjuster / doctor / legal specialist), not documentation.

document_type.description: Standard document-type code. See pipeline.md's taxonomy section.

decision_type.description: Insurer decision for one claim coverage or claim item. denial means no payment for that item; reduction means payment liability is recognized but the payable amount is reduced. A case may contain separate reasons of both types.

payment_status.description: Observed payment outcome, kept separate from decision_type. No automatic decision_type-to-payment_status rule is enforced until enough real data exists.

taxonomy_code.description: Denial/reduction-reason R-code. See pipeline.md's taxonomy section. x-codebook carries the loss-adjuster-reviewed Korean label, observed frequency tier, and applicable decision types. These are taxonomy metadata, not case evidence or model confidence. An empty applicable_decision_types array means the adjuster's source table left the classification blank; it does not mean the code can never apply.

R20.label_ko: 계약 전 알릴의무 위반에 따른 비례감액

prompt_version.description: Recorded to tie evaluation results to a specific prompt version. e.g. field_extraction_v0.1

evidence_reference.description: A reference to a source quote. document_id may be omitted when the document context is already unambiguous (e.g. inside a per-document array).

strict_evidence_reference.description: An evidence reference whose SOURCE LOCATION is mandatory rather than optional. Same shape as evidence_reference, but document_id and page are required too, so the reference always resolves back to a specific page of a specific document. Used by the denial/reduction family (denial_reason_result grounds and policy matches, denial_validation_result), where a reason nobody can trace to a page is the exact failure P1 exists to prevent -- an adjuster rebutting an insurer has to be able to point at where it says that. evidence_reference remains the looser default for contracts whose surrounding structure already fixes the document context; do not globally swap one for the other.
